paragraphs: Master Product + Recommendation System Blueprint
DOC-01 | Version 1.0 | Meal Planning RE Technical Docs v1
1. Purpose and Scope

Defines the full product and recommendation-engine blueprint: how onboarding, household profile, cohort assignment, weekly class-first planning, add-ons, dish expansion, Food DNA scoring, feedback, APIs, analytics, and admin operations fit together.

This document is part of the canonical 28-document technical system. It should be read in the order defined in DOC-00. It is written for an AI coding agent that must implement the agreed recommendation engine exactly, without inventing a competing architecture.
2. Applicable Build Series
3. Core Implementation Invariants
Always build a household profile before generating any plan.
Always map user-facing cohort answers into backend personas and overlays; do not expose all backend personas in onboarding.
Always generate a 7-day class-first plan before selecting dishes.
Always keep primary family meal classes separate from member-specific add-on components.
Never allow kid, elderly, pregnancy, postpartum, diabetic, or recovery add-on classes to replace the main family meal unless the household itself has only that member segment.
Always apply hard constraints before scoring: diet type, allergies, Jain/fasting rules, and Never list.
Always use home state and current city as separate signals; do not collapse them into one location field.
Always use class-to-dish mapping keyed by meal_class_code; never mix dishes from unrelated meal classes.
Use v3 workbook IDs as canonical seed IDs unless a later approved source-data version supersedes them.
4. Product Problem and Design Principle

Users often self-report aspirational dishes, while real behavior lives at meal-class level. Therefore the engine must understand classes such as simple green vegetable sabzi, dal-rice comfort, stuffed flatbread breakfast, light grain breakfast, fish curry rice, and weekend festive breakfast. The product should feel magical because a few onboarding answers produce a realistic household week.
5. End-to-End Architecture
Capture minimal onboarding answers through dynamic cohort routing.
Normalize answers into household profile fields.
Assign main cohort, sub-cohort, backend persona, overlays, diet mode, cook mode, and confidence.
Generate class-first weekly plan using cohort matrix and state/city overlays.
Attach member-specific add-ons for infant, toddler, child, elderly, pregnancy, postpartum, diabetic, recovery, and fitness needs.
Expand classes into dish candidates from class-specific pools.
Rank dish candidates using Food DNA, constraints, day context, city/state signals, and feedback history.
Learn from revealed behavior and update preference vectors.
6. Core Objects
7. Recommendation Engine Versions
8. System Boundaries

This blueprint covers planning and recommendation. It does not require nutrition-grade medical advice, automatic grocery fulfillment, or fully generative recipes in MVP. Health/lifecycle overlays are planning aids and constraints, not medical diagnosis.
Class-first planning is mandatory for MVP.
Dish-level scoring can be simple initially but must use the same data model.
The source workbook v3 is enough for seed data; later admin operations can update it through governed releases.
9. Worked Examples
MP family in Mumbai

A user says they are planning for a family, originally from Madhya Pradesh, living in Mumbai, vegetarian, with an infant and diabetic elder. The system should not ask them to choose from 41 personas. It infers family + infant + diabetic overlay, uses MP home-style meals, adds Mumbai Tier 1 lifestyle influence, creates primary family classes, then attaches infant and diabetic add-ons.
10. AI Coding Agent Instructions
Read DOC-00 first to understand build sequence and document dependencies.
Use this document when implementing: BUILD-00, BUILD-01, BUILD-02, BUILD-03, BUILD-04, BUILD-05, BUILD-06, BUILD-07, BUILD-08, BUILD-09, BUILD-10.
Treat all IDs in source workbook v3 as canonical seed data identifiers.
When logic and data conflict, follow the rule hierarchy: hard constraints > add-on separation > weekly class matrix > state/city overlay > dish scoring > randomization.
Do not create new meal class codes, persona codes, or enum values in application code. Additions must go through DOC-27 Admin CMS and DOC-28 governance.
Write unit tests based on the acceptance criteria in this document and DOC-25.
11. Acceptance Criteria
A developer can explain the complete chain from onboarding answer to ranked dish card.
The implementation can be split into build modules without ambiguity.
All future docs can reference this blueprint as the product source of truth.
12. Change Log
tables: BUILD-00, BUILD-01, BUILD-02, BUILD-03, BUILD-04, BUILD-05, BUILD-06, BUILD-07, BUILD-08, BUILD-09, BUILD-10

Defines the full product and recommendation-engine blueprint: how onboarding, household profile, cohort assignment, weekly class-first planning, add-ons, dish expansion, Food DNA scoring, feedback, APIs, analytics, and admin operations fit together.
Claude / AI coding agent, product owner, backend developer, data operations, QA
Generate meal classes first; expand to dishes second; attach member add-ons separately.
Create canonical docs, read order, source workbook contract, and non-negotiable architecture rules.
Create DB schema, load source tables, enforce IDs, constraints, and versioned seed data.
Build dynamic onboarding, household/member capture, diet and city inputs, and profile object.
Map profile answers to main cohort, sub-cohort, backend persona, state/city overlay, non-veg mode, and confidence.
Generate 7-day meal-class plan from cohort matrix, regional rules, city overlay, weekday/weekend rhythm, variety rules.
Attach infant/toddler/child/elderly/pregnancy/postpartum/diabetic/fitness add-ons without replacing family primary meal.
Dish Expansion and Food DNA Ranking
Expand selected meal classes into dish candidate pools and rank dishes using Food DNA + constraints + context.
Use swipes, locks, Not Today, Never, search, and accepted repeats to adjust preferences and class weights.
Expose onboarding/profile/plan/generation/feedback endpoints with stable JSON contracts.
Build admin workflows to maintain classes, dishes, add-ons, Food DNA tags, data QA, and releases.
Track funnel, plan quality, acceptance, add-on relevance, class accuracy, taxonomy changes, and A/B tests.
Normalized object produced from onboarding answers, device/current city, home state, diet, cook dependency, household members.
Detailed machine-facing persona selected from v3 persona master. Not shown as a long list to users.
Behavioral eating category, such as simple sabzi, dal-rice, stuffed flatbread, fish curry rice.
7 days x meal slots x primary/secondary/tertiary meal classes.
Extra member-specific dish/component that does not replace main family meal.
A dish option inside the selected meal class pool.
Dish-level attributes for scoring and personalization.
Rule-based class-first planning from onboarding, cohort matrix, region/city, diet, weekday/weekend, cook mode, add-ons.
Adds acceptance, rejection, repeat tolerance, Never/Not Today, class affinity drift, Food DNA preference learning.
Seeds new users from similar Food DNA clusters; still respects all RE-v1/RE-v2 constraints.
Personal history plus cluster trends, richer context, pantry, health targets, and advanced collaborative filtering.
Initial canonical technical document generated from agreed v3 workbook and documentation architecture.
